concepts.finding: NN techniques are classified into structureless (Weighted kNN, Condensed NN, Reduced NN) and structure-based (k-d tree, Ball tree, Principal Axis Tree).
concepts.implication: Comprehensive taxonomy provides a reference for selecting appropriate search algorithms based on data characteristics (dimensionality, size, distribution).
concepts.open_question: Which NN technique is optimal for query-to-document matching in web-scale search?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: For high-dimensional query embeddings (>100 dims), graph-based methods (HNSW) will consistently outperform tree-based methods (k-d tree) by 10x+ in throughput at equivalent recall.
